sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Stellus Capital Investment Corporation Announces $0.34 First Quarter 2026 Regular Dividend, Payable Monthly in Increments of $0.1133 in February, March, and April 2026
feed.summary: Stellus Capital Investment Corporation announced a first-quarter 2026 regular dividend of $0.34 per share, payable monthly in increments of $0.1133 in February, March, and April 2026. The CEO noted that the dividend reduction reflects the current lower interest rate environment. The company's investment objective is to maximize total return through investments in private middle-market companies.

feed.title: International Paper declares $0.4625 dividend
feed.summary: International Paper (IP) has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.4625 per share. This dividend is payable on March 15, 2023, to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2023.

feed.title: State Street Corp. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: This article states that State Street Corp. stock underperformed on Friday when compared to its competitors. It provides no further details on the specific performance metrics or the competitors it's being compared against.

feed.title: MGM Resorts Approves Long-Term Compensation Package for CLO - TipRanks.com
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International has approved a new employment agreement for its Chief Legal and Administrative Officer and Secretary, John McManus, effective January 1, 2026, through December 31, 2029. The package includes a minimum annual base salary of $1 million, an annual target bonus of 150% of base salary, and significant equity-based compensation. This move aims to retain senior legal leadership, align incentives with long-term shareholder value, and ensure corporate stability.

feed.title: Fastenal declares quarterly dividend of $0.24 per share By Investing.com
feed.summary: Fastenal Company announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.24 per share, payable on February 26, 2026. This dividend reflects an 11.6% increase from the previous year and continues the company's 33-year streak of dividend payments. Fastenal will report its next quarterly earnings on January 20, 2026, and has recently announced a CEO transition.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan receives imminent danger order at Morenci mine in Arizona
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan's subsidiary, Freeport-McMoRan Morenci Inc., received an imminent danger order from MSHA at its Morenci mine in Arizona due to employees working on an elevated conveyor belt without fall protection. The company promptly addressed the issue, and the order was terminated the same day. This incident follows recent positive financial news for Freeport-McMoRan, including stronger-than-expected Q3 2025 earnings and positive analyst ratings.

feed.title: Altria Group (MO) Stock Rallies Amid Strong Analyst Ratings
feed.summary: Altria Group (MO) has seen a significant stock price increase, outperforming the market, partly due to the FDA's approval of its on! Plus nicotine pouches. Despite mixed analyst ratings and concerns about liquidity and leverage, the company shows strong profitability and financial stability. Its attractive 7.5% dividend yield continues to appeal to income-focused investors, though some valuation metrics suggest potential overvaluation and a high dividend payout ratio raises sustainability questions.

feed.title: ROK Rockwell Automation (NYSE) After Hours $415.39 16 Jan 2026: AI boost
feed.summary: Rockwell Automation (ROK) is trading after hours at $415.39, down 1.36%, as investors consider its upcoming earnings and strong AI-driven demand for automation. The company is highlighted as a top industrial AI play with solid profitability, though its valuation is perceived as rich. Meyka AI rates ROK as a "BUY" but projects near-term downside.

feed.title: AES flags up to $325 million impairment charge on Bulgaria power plant
feed.summary: AES Corporation announced an impairment charge of up to $325 million related to its Maritza power plant in Bulgaria. This charge comes as the Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) for the plant is set to expire in May 2026. The company operates globally with significant assets in electricity production and distribution across various countries.

feed.title: KeyCorp director retires, board size reduced to 14
feed.summary: KeyCorp announced that director David K. Wilson retired from its Board of Directors on January 13, 2026, due to personal health reasons. Following his departure, the board reduced its size to 14 members, effective January 15, 2026. An analyst rating on KeyCorp (KEY) stock is a Hold with a $24.00 price target, and TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates KEY as Neutral, highlighting improved financial performance but also concerns about valuation and overbought technicals.

feed.title: BlackRock to Change Primary Listing Venue for Four iShares ETFs
feed.summary: BlackRock announced that four iShares exchange-traded funds (ETFs) will change their primary listing venue from NYSE Arca and Nasdaq to the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) on or around February 23, 2026. The affected ETFs include iShares Government Money Market ETF (GMMF), iShares Prime Money Market ETF (PMMF), iShares 0-3 Month Treasury Bond ETF (SGOV), and iShares 0–1 Year Treasury Bond ETF (SHV). This change is intended to provide investors with consistent market quality and liquidity, with no action required from current shareholders.

feed.title: Fastenal Company Announces Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (Nasdaq:FAST) has announced that its board of directors declared a cash dividend of $0.24 per share, payable on February 26, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 29, 2026. The company has a history of paying regular quarterly dividends since 2011, alongside occasional special dividends. The board intends to continue quarterly dividend payments, subject to the company's financial performance and other relevant factors.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin, GE Aerospace demo novel ramjet engine for hypersonic weapons
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin and GE Aerospace have successfully demonstrated a liquid-fueled rotating detonation ramjet engine, a novel propulsion system for hypersonic missiles. This technology aims to enable missiles to fly farther and reach speeds of at least Mach 5 more affordably than current options. The companies plan further maturation throughout 2026, leveraging GE's rotating detonation combustion and Lockheed Martin's tactical inlet to improve engine performance and efficiency.

feed.title: J.M. Smucker declares $1.10 per share quarterly dividend
feed.summary: The J.M. Smucker Co. (NYSE:SJM) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.10 per share, maintaining its 55-year dividend payment streak and 16 consecutive years of dividend increases. The dividend will be paid on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 13, 2026, offering a dividend yield of 4.2% based on the current share price. The company, known for brands like Folgers and Jif, recently reported mixed Q2 earnings, prompting varied analyst reactions regarding its financial outlook.

feed.title: The Truth About Gilead Sciences: Is This ‘Boring’ Pharma Stock About To Blow Up?
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences, a large-cap pharma company known for HIV and antiviral treatments, is making a significant push into cancer therapies. While it's not a high-hype stock, it offers a solid dividend yield and stable cash flow, making it attractive to long-term investors seeking defensive plays and income. The article discusses Gilead's financial strength, its oncology pipeline as a potential growth driver, and how it compares to competitors, advising investors to consider their goals (hype vs. value) when evaluating the stock.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Announces an Investigation Against Teleflex Incorporated (TFX) and Encourages Stockholders to Learn More About the Investigation
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has launched an investigation into Teleflex Incorporated (TFX) following a significant drop in its stock price. This decline occurred after the unexpected departure of Teleflex's Chairman, President, and CEO, Liam Kelly, on January 8, 2026. The law firm is encouraging investors who purchased Teleflex securities to join the investigation into potential claims.

feed.title: Google stock price: Alphabet Class C (GOOG) slips after-hours on data-sharing court fight — here’s what’s next
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) dipped approximately 0.8% in after-hours trading following Google's request to delay a court order mandating data sharing with rivals in a U.S. search monopoly lawsuit. The company warns that compliance could reveal trade secrets, while attention also turns to a potential YouTube-BBC deal and Alphabet's upcoming earnings report on February 4th. This legal development adds uncertainty for investors as courts focus on remedies for the monopoly ruling.

feed.title: RBC Global Asset Management files for ETF share class relief, signaling entry into U.S. market
feed.summary: RBC Global Asset Management has filed for ETF share class relief, indicating its intention to enter the U.S. market. This move suggests the Canadian firm is looking to expand its exchange-traded fund offerings into the United States. The article highlights this development as a significant step for RBC GAM.

feed.title: AMETEK Inc.: The Quiet Powerhouse Defining the Future of Industrial Electronics
feed.summary: AMETEK Inc. is positioned as a foundational technology layer in industrial markets, specializing in precision instrumentation and advanced motion control. The company's strategy involves integrating high-margin, mission-critical hardware and software across various industrial sectors, making it a "hidden giant" influencing aerospace, electric vehicles, and semiconductor manufacturing. AMETEK Inc. attracts investors due to its disciplined acquisition strategy, high-value niche positioning, and alignment with major industrial megatrends, resulting in resilient financial performance and a strong competitive moat.

feed.title: Metropolitan Bank Holding increases quarterly dividend to $0.20 per share By Investing.com
feed.summary: Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. announced a quarterly cash dividend increase to $0.20 per share, up from $0.15, yielding 0.73% at the current share price. The dividend is payable on February 6, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 27, 2026. This announcement follows recent industry recognitions and the bank's strategic moves, despite a significant EPS miss in Q3 2025.

feed.title: Apple stock slips after India antitrust warning; AAPL traders brace for key dates
feed.summary: Apple Inc. shares slipped after an antitrust warning from India's competition watchdog regarding its App Store policies, potentially leading to significant fines. The Delhi High Court will revisit Apple's challenge on January 27, while investors also await Apple's quarterly earnings release on January 29. Additionally, Apple and Google are being pressured by advocacy groups to remove X and Grok from their app stores due to concerns over sexually explicit AI-generated images.

feed.title: Micron breaks ground on work to construct semiconductor-manufacturing campus in Clay
feed.summary: Micron Technology has officially broken ground on its $100 billion semiconductor-manufacturing campus in Clay, New York. This project, which will be the largest semiconductor facility in the U.S. with up to four fabrication facilities, is expected to create 50,000 jobs. Production is slated to begin in 2030, with early work focused on clearing land by the end of March to accommodate endangered bat species.

feed.title: ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF Options Spot-On: On January 16th, 319.36K Contracts Were Traded, With 1.65 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: On January 16th, the ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF (TQQQ.US) experienced significant options trading volume, totaling 319.36K contracts. Call options accounted for 60.86% of transactions, indicating a bullish sentiment. The open interest for TQQQ.US reached approximately 1.65 million contracts, representing 98.61% of the 30-day average.

feed.title: The protein boom: Starbucks, Subway and beyond load up menus
feed.summary: Driven by increasing consumer demand for healthier options and the influence of weight-loss drugs, restaurants like Starbucks, Subway, Sweetgreen, and Cava are significantly expanding their high-protein menu offerings. Subway launched new Protein Pockets, Starbucks rolled out protein cold foam and lattes, Sweetgreen introduced a 106-gram protein bowl, and Cava plans to add more protein items like salmon. This trend signifies a broader shift in the food industry toward prioritizing protein quantity, nutrient quality, and functionality.

feed.title: Why PNC Financial Services Group (PNC) Stock Is Up Today
feed.summary: PNC Financial Services Group (PNC) stock rose 3.8% after reporting better-than-expected fourth-quarter results, with EPS of $4.88 against an estimated $4.21 and revenue of $6.07 billion surpassing projections of $5.97 billion. The strong performance, including favorable banking metrics, boosted investor confidence, leading to a new 52-week high for the shares. The company's shares are up 5.5% since the beginning of the year.

feed.title: Lionsgate Studios (NYSE:LION) Sets New 52-Week High - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Lionsgate Studios (NYSE:LION) recently hit a new 52-week high of $9.50 on heavy trading volume, closing up about 3.1%. Despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts with an average price target of $9.14, the company recorded a negative P/E ratio and missed quarterly EPS estimates. An insider sold a significant number of shares, while institutional investors have adjusted their holdings.

feed.title: Is QuidelOrtho (QDEL) Starting To Look Interesting After Its Recent Share Price Rebound?
feed.summary: QuidelOrtho (QDEL) has seen its share price rebound recently, with a 5.6% gain over 7 days and 22.5% over 30 days, despite a 15.5% decline over the last year. Simply Wall St's analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, with a Discounted Cash Flow model indicating it could be 61.2% below its intrinsic value and a Price/Sales ratio also pointing to undervaluation compared to its "Fair Ratio." The article encourages investors to consider these valuation metrics in light of the company's position in healthcare diagnostics.

feed.title: Paychex, Inc. (NASDAQ:PAYX) Declares $1.08 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Paychex, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $1.08 per share, payable on February 27th to shareholders of record on January 28th, with an ex-dividend date also on January 28th. This amounts to an annualized payout of $4.32, yielding approximately 3.9%. While the dividend is currently covered by earnings at a 76.2% payout ratio, analysts project earnings per share of $5.31 next year, potentially increasing the payout ratio to 81.4%, suggesting limited cushion for future earnings declines.

feed.title: Adams Natural Resources Fund, Inc. (NYSE:PEO) Announces $0.49 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Adams Natural Resources Fund, Inc. (NYSE:PEO) declared a quarterly dividend of $0.49 per share, to be paid on February 25th to shareholders of record on January 26th, offering an 8.4% annualized yield. Following the announcement, PEO shares traded up approximately 0.5% to $23.46 on increased volume, near its 12-month high. The fund, a closed-end management company, provides diversified exposure to natural resources and energy equities.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines Q4 Preview: Not A Buy After The Run-Up From The Lows (NYSE:LUV)
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) is set to report its Q4 and full-year earnings on January 29th. Despite anticipated profitability improvements, modest capacity growth, and potential fuel price relief in 2026, the stock remains expensive. The estimated fair value of $27.40 is below current levels, leading the author to remain on the sidelines due to the stock's valuation after its recent run-up.

feed.title: D.R. Horton Shareholders Reaffirm Board, Pay and Auditor
feed.summary: D.R. Horton shareholders re-elected eight directors and approved executive compensation at their January 15, 2026 annual meeting, also reaffirming Ernst & Young as their auditor for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026. Despite a "Hold" analyst rating and concerns about declining sales prices and high cancellation rates, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates DHI as "Neutral," citing solid financial health and reasonable valuation alongside bearish technical indicators. D.R. Horton is a major U.S. homebuilding company with a current market cap of $46.53 billion.

feed.title: The J.M. Smucker Co. Declares Dividend
feed.summary: The J.M. Smucker Co. announced that its Board of Directors has approved a dividend of $1.10 per share on the company's common shares. This dividend will be distributed on Monday, March 2, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of the close of business on Friday, February 13, 2026. The company remains committed to providing quality food products for people and pets under a diverse family of brands.

feed.title: Sempra and Shell face off in Mexico over pipeline award
feed.summary: Sempra's subsidiary, Gasoducto de Aguaprieta, is challenging an ICC award in Mexico City that requires it to pay $127 million to Shell Mexico Gas Natural for the early termination of a natural gas transportation contract. Shell, in turn, has initiated enforcement proceedings and requested the attachment of Aguaprieta's bank accounts. This arbitration follows a previous dispute where Shell was ordered to pay Sempra's subsidiary over $600 million related to storage contracts at the same Costa Azul facility.

feed.title: RiverNorth Opportunities Fund, Inc. and RiverNorth/DoubleLine Strategic Opportunity Fund, Inc. Announce Preferred Dividends
feed.summary: RiverNorth Opportunities Fund, Inc. and RiverNorth/DoubleLine Strategic Opportunity Fund, Inc. have announced the declaration of preferred dividends for the first quarter of 2026. The funds specified ex-dates, record dates, and payable dates, along with distribution per share for various series of preferred stocks. These distributions are calculated based on the preferred shares' liquidation preference and current quarterly distribution rates.

feed.title: Puerto Rico’s OFG Bancorp sets Jan. 22 call on 2025 results
feed.summary: OFG Bancorp will report its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results on Thursday, January 22, 2026, before the market opens. The company will also host a conference call at 10:00 AM ET on the same day to discuss the results. Interested parties can access the call via a toll-free number or a live webcast on OFG Bancorp's website.

feed.title: BlackRock Offers Senior Executives Carry Pay for Private Markets
feed.summary: BlackRock is introducing new pay perks for its senior executives, including carried interest tied to the performance of its private funds. This move signals BlackRock's aggressive strategy to expand its presence in private markets and directly compete with leading alternative asset managers. The firm announced these changes in a recent regulatory filing.

feed.title: Macerich’s Stock On The Edge: Is This Retail REIT Quietly Setting Up Its Next Move?
feed.summary: Macerich's stock has recently declined, reflecting investor caution despite the company's efforts in redevelopment and debt reduction. While the stock has seen a slight downward trend over the past five sessions, a one-year view shows a respectable, albeit volatile, gain due to balance sheet repair and improved perceptions of Class A malls. Analysts are cautiously optimistic, with most firms issuing "Hold" ratings and price targets modestly above the current share price, pending future interest rate movements and consumer spending.

feed.title: 2 Growth Stocks to Invest $1,000 in Right Now
feed.summary: This article identifies Opendoor (NASDAQ: OPEN) and Nextpower (NASDAQ: NXT) as two growth stocks with significant upside potential. It suggests that Opendoor could rebound as the housing market stabilizes and its new strategies take hold, while Nextpower, transitioning into a diversified energy technology company, stands to benefit from the growing demand for renewable energy and AI integration. Both companies are presented as speculative but potentially lucrative long-term investments for patient investors.

feed.title: Ondas Holdings Faces Steep Decline Amid Insider Stock Sell-Off
feed.summary: Ondas Holdings Inc. (ONDS) experienced a significant stock decline of over 5% following the revelation of an insider selling shares worth approximately $4.61 million. This sell-off, coupled with the company's negative EBITDA and pre-tax profit margins, has fueled investor uncertainty and market volatility. Despite high liquidity, Ondas Holdings faces challenges in profitability and rebuilding investor trust amidst these financial headwinds.

feed.title: Why ManpowerGroup (MAN) Stock Is Falling Today
feed.summary: ManpowerGroup's (MAN) stock fell 3.1% after UBS lowered its price target to $32 from $39, maintaining a 'Neutral' rating due to a subdued outlook and expected "somewhat messy bottoming" process for the shares. This cautious view follows a projection of Manpower's fourth-quarter earnings per share slightly below market consensus, attributed to an anticipated decline in profit margins amid a weakening job market. The drop contrasts with a recent upgrade to Outperform by BMO Capital, which saw an attractive entry point for staffing stocks.

feed.title: Western Digital price target raised to $230 from $190 at Evercore ISI
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has raised its price target for Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) to $230 from $190, maintaining an Outperform rating due to strong end-market demand and a favorable pricing environment. The firm expects Western Digital to monetize its remaining 5% stake in SanDisk, using the proceeds to reduce debt and return capital to shareholders. This move, combined with a shift towards higher capacity drives, is anticipated to boost earnings per share.

feed.title: Crocs Inc and Home Depot Inc Options Show Significant Trading Volume
feed.summary: Crocs Inc. and Home Depot Inc. both experienced significant options trading volume, signaling strong investor interest. Crocs' options volume reached 5,806 contracts, with notable bullish sentiment seen in the $87.50 strike call option, while Home Depot's volume hit 19,921 contracts, reflecting optimism for its $395 strike call option. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" rating for Crocs, with an average 1-year price target of $89.71.

feed.title: Why Caesars Entertainment (CZR) Shares Are Sliding Today
feed.summary: Shares of Caesars Entertainment (CZR) fell 3.3% after Morgan Stanley lowered its price target on the stock to $27 from $29, while maintaining an "Equal-Weight" rating. This adjustment reflects diminished optimism about the stock's future, adding to a challenging five-year period during which the stock's value declined significantly. The company's shares are up 3.2% year-to-date, but still trade 38.9% below their 52-week high.

feed.title: Caterpillar, Nvidia Partner to Combine Heavy Industry With Physical AI
feed.summary: Caterpillar and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to integrate AI across heavy industries. This partnership aims to create AI-enhanced customer solutions and manufacturing systems using Nvidia's Jetson Thor platform for real-time AI inference on equipment and developing the Cat AI Assistant. Caterpillar is also leveraging Nvidia's AI Factory and Omniverse for manufacturing and supply chain transformation, including digital twins to optimize production processes.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $90,000 of BALL CORPORATION lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: BALL CORPORATION recently disclosed $90,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, focusing on aluminum can recycling, packaging labeling, and related legislation. The report also details insider trading activity, showing one sale by the CEO, and significant institutional investor movements in BALL stock. Furthermore, recent analyst ratings include several "Buy" and "Outperform" recommendations with a median price target of $61.0.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $70,000 of AVERY DENNISON CORPORATION lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: A lobbying disclosure reported $70,000 of AVERY DENNISON CORPORATION (AVY) lobbying from Q4 2025, specifically on issues related to "FCC Petition for rulemaking." The article also details recent hedge fund activity, showing significant adds and drops in AVY shares by institutional investors. Furthermore, recent analyst ratings and price targets for AVY are provided, indicating a generally positive outlook with a median price target of $203.5.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $60,000 of AMGEN INC. FKA HORIZON THERAPEUTICS USA INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. (formerly Horizon Therapeutics USA Inc.) disclosed $60,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, focusing on bills like H.R. 3 and S. 102 related to drug pricing and healthcare research. The article also details recent congressional stock trades, insider trading activity, hedge fund movements, and analyst ratings/price targets for AMGN.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $122,000 of T. ROWE PRICE GROUP INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price Group Inc. disclosed $122,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, focusing on retirement-related legislation through the "GROWTH Act," "Retirement Fairness for Charities and Education Institutions Act," and the "INVEST Act." The article also details recent insider trading sales by TROW executives, significant institutional investor activity in Q3 2025, and current analyst ratings with price targets for the company's stock.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $170,000 of KLA CORPORATION lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: KLA Corporation disclosed $170,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, focusing on trade policy with China, semiconductor tariffs, and R&D funding. The article also provides details on congressional stock trading, insider transactions, hedge fund activity, and recent analyst ratings and price targets for KLAC stock.

feed.title: Active ETFs Face New Cost Pressure As Schwab Weighs Distribution Fees, JPMorgan Says
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co predicts that actively managed ETFs will soon face increased distribution costs as Charles Schwab Corporation plans to implement a fee system for ETF issuers to access its platform. This new fee structure could generate an estimated $500 million in revenue for Schwab, with actively managed ETFs being the primary target due to their higher management fees compared to passive funds. The move is part of Schwab's "third-party-payer" strategy, which is expected to boost its earnings.
